### [Procurement process automation @ 0:27](https://fathom.video/share/z4UJeEdc7Ycwm1KkbPFbsBs_FNsUKogX?tab=summary&timestamp=27)

The team is designing a procurement process where product details are automatically captured by scanning a barcode, eliminating the need for manual data entry. This will streamline the receiving and inventory processes.

### [Recipe and production planning @ 0:59](https://fathom.video/share/z4UJeEdc7Ycwm1KkbPFbsBs_FNsUKogX?tab=summary&timestamp=59)

The team discussed the recipe and production planning for a specific product, including the raw material inputs, expected yields, and the process of converting the raw materials into the final packaged product. They need to determine whether to plan based on raw or finished weights, and how to account for shrinkage during cooking.

### [Packaging and packing decisions @ 6:59](https://fathom.video/share/z4UJeEdc7Ycwm1KkbPFbsBs_FNsUKogX?tab=summary&timestamp=419)

After the cooking and drying steps, the team will need to decide on the final packaging sizes and quantities for the 150kg of finished product, such as 1kg, 500g, or smaller packs. This will involve coordinating the slicing, packing, and labeling steps.

### [Sub-recipe management @ 9:29](https://fathom.video/share/z4UJeEdc7Ycwm1KkbPFbsBs_FNsUKogX?tab=summary&timestamp=569)

The team discussed the relationship between the main recipe and the sub-recipes, and the need to create and stock the sub-recipes before starting the main production plan. This ensures the necessary components are available when needed.

### [Recap and next steps @ 12:49](https://fathom.video/share/z4UJeEdc7Ycwm1KkbPFbsBs_FNsUKogX?tab=summary&timestamp=769)

The team summarized the key points, confirming the need to first create and stock the sub-recipes, then use those as inputs to the main production plan. No further questions were raised, and the meeting concluded.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
### [Encounter type and category mapping @ 0:00](https://fathom.video/share/u5e14Pp5FL4bGvLetxyzGiy2ht2knAMP?tab=summary&timestamp=0)

The team discussed setting up a system to map encounter types to encounter categories and outcomes. They explored options for using dropdown fields or linked fields to connect the encounter type to the appropriate category and outcome. The goal is to ensure the correct category and status code are automatically populated based on the selected encounter type.

### [Handling mandatory fields @ 9:00](https://fathom.video/share/u5e14Pp5FL4bGvLetxyzGiy2ht2knAMP?tab=summary&timestamp=540)

The group addressed the challenge of mandatory fields, noting that certain fields must be selected in order for the system to function properly. They discussed potential workarounds, such as making fields temporarily non-mandatory, to avoid blockers when entering new data.

### [Populating status and activity date @ 11:00](https://fathom.video/share/u5e14Pp5FL4bGvLetxyzGiy2ht2knAMP?tab=summary&timestamp=660)

The team reviewed the logic for populating the status and activity date fields. They confirmed that the status should automatically update based on the selected outcome, and the activity date should default to the current date, with the ability to manually override it if needed.

### [Finalizing the data model @ 15:00](https://fathom.video/share/u5e14Pp5FL4bGvLetxyzGiy2ht2knAMP?tab=summary&timestamp=900)

The discussion focused on finalizing the data model, including the relationships between encounter type, category, outcome, and status. The group explored options for using linked or dropdown fields to ensure data consistency and ease of use when entering new records.

### [Next steps and testing @ 18:00](https://fathom.video/share/u5e14Pp5FL4bGvLetxyzGiy2ht2knAMP?tab=summary&timestamp=1080)

The team agreed to finalize the data field configuration, either using a linked or dropdown approach. Avinash will test the updated setup and provide feedback. They also discussed plans to address the remaining "spells" functionality in a future session.

### [Tracking barcodes and supplier details @ 0:03](https://fathom.video/share/isQ2VXAxrnPjsjVafskmxrbNMH-JzWC8?tab=summary&timestamp=3)

The discussion focused on understanding the information contained in the barcodes on brisket product stickers, including the tracking number, weight, and packing date. The participants explored how this information could be used to link the product back to the supplier.

### [Handling multiple suppliers for the same product @ 2:50](https://fathom.video/share/isQ2VXAxrnPjsjVafskmxrbNMH-JzWC8?tab=summary&timestamp=170)

The team discussed the approach for managing brisket products from different suppliers. The recommendation was to create multiple variants of the "beef brisket" raw material, each linked to a specific supplier, to maintain traceability and enable FIFO inventory management.

### [Generating custom serial numbers @ 4:07](https://fathom.video/share/isQ2VXAxrnPjsjVafskmxrbNMH-JzWC8?tab=summary&timestamp=247)

The participants considered whether creating custom serial numbers based on packing date and weight would be helpful, but determined that was unnecessary since the existing tracking numbers could be used to manage inventory and picking.

### [Ensuring accurate picking and FIFO @ 5:06](https://fathom.video/share/isQ2VXAxrnPjsjVafskmxrbNMH-JzWC8?tab=summary&timestamp=306)

The group discussed how the warehouse team could use the tracking numbers to accurately pick the correct batch of brisket, ensuring FIFO inventory management. They agreed to test the process to confirm it works as expected.

### [Next steps @ 7:00](https://fathom.video/share/isQ2VXAxrnPjsjVafskmxrbNMH-JzWC8?tab=summary&timestamp=420)

Awais agreed to provide Tim with additional sample labels from different suppliers so they could further analyze the barcode information and ensure a robust process for managing brisket inventory from multiple sources.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
### [Tracking sticker with barcode creation @ 0:04](https://fathom.video/share/7nRdnKyy6zyUs5ZUV6RPgyGm9jyvFXyt?tab=summary&timestamp=4)

The team discussed the creation of a tracking sticker with a barcode that would contain key information about the product, including the product name, production date, batch quantity, and production instructions. They agreed that this sticker would be an important part of the production process.

### [Job cards and critical control points @ 2:21](https://fathom.video/share/7nRdnKyy6zyUs5ZUV6RPgyGm9jyvFXyt?tab=summary&timestamp=141)

The group talked about breaking down the production process into specific "job cards" that would capture details like temperature, cooking time, and the employee responsible for each step. They emphasized the importance of having clear instructions and critical control points documented in these job cards.

### [Inventory management and weight reconciliation @ 3:27](https://fathom.video/share/7nRdnKyy6zyUs5ZUV6RPgyGm9jyvFXyt?tab=summary&timestamp=207)

The team discussed the process of capturing the weight of raw materials from the supplier or internal inventory, and ensuring that the weight matches the production plan. They agreed on the need for an admin override process if the weight differs from the plan, and for updating the inventory system accordingly.

### [Project scope and timeline @ 18:57](https://fathom.video/share/7nRdnKyy6zyUs5ZUV6RPgyGm9jyvFXyt?tab=summary&timestamp=1137)

The team reviewed the planned scope and timeline for the first phase of the project, focusing on setting up the item master data, procurement process, production planning, and packing. They agreed to share additional details via email and schedule follow-up meetings as needed.

### [Packing, labeling, and storage @ 21:46](https://fathom.video/share/7nRdnKyy6zyUs5ZUV6RPgyGm9jyvFXyt?tab=summary&timestamp=1306)

As the final step, the group covered the packing process, including the use of the DG700 scale to capture variable weights, the creation of product labels with storage instructions (chilled vs. frozen), and the tracking of the total batch weight on the outer case label.

### [Establishing product specifications @ 0:00](https://fathom.video/share/atwRskMPsFKQn2PiwAz5yPx5sMSyWSFx?tab=summary&timestamp=0)

The team discussed how product specifications and certifications are obtained - the raw material details and allergen information come from the supplier database, while the final product testing and certification is done by a third-party laboratory. They agreed that each recipe/final product will have its own associated certification that includes all the necessary nutritional and allergen details.

### [Handling raw material inventory @ 5:03](https://fathom.video/share/atwRskMPsFKQn2PiwAz5yPx5sMSyWSFx?tab=summary&timestamp=303)

The team covered how raw materials from suppliers are received, scanned, and tracked in the system. Each item has a unique serial number that is captured, allowing the ability to trace back through the supply chain. The system should be able to automatically pull the weight and other details from the supplier barcodes.

### [Production planning and tracking @ 14:51](https://fathom.video/share/atwRskMPsFKQn2PiwAz5yPx5sMSyWSFx?tab=summary&timestamp=891)

When production is planned, a unique batch number is generated that links to the specific raw materials used. This batch number is then used to track the final products produced, with each individual item also having a unique serial number. This allows traceability back to the specific raw material sources if any issues arise with the final product.

### [Next steps @ 42:40](https://fathom.video/share/atwRskMPsFKQn2PiwAz5yPx5sMSyWSFx?tab=summary&timestamp=2560)

The team agreed to continue the discussion on the specific details of the production tracking system, including how to handle partial quantities from inventory, managing sub-recipes, and the labeling/documentation requirements. They will provide more sample product data to test the proposed solution.
